title: Fudgy brownies
subtitle: Dark chocolate brownies with a crackled top and dense centre
excerpt: Dense, fudgy dark chocolate brownies made with melted chocolate and butter rather than cocoa alone. The batter is whisked quickly (no creaming, no beating) and baked at low heat until the surface is set and the centre is still soft. The centre firms as the brownies cool. Cut into 16.
sourceNotes: Chocolate brownies are documented in American domestic cookbooks from the late nineteenth century, many available through the Internet Archive. The combination of melted chocolate, butter, sugar, and eggs as the base for a dense, fudgy baked square is documented in American household baking manuals from the 1890s onward. The fudgy, underbaked style (pulled from the oven while the centre is still soft) is distinguished from cakier versions in domestic baking literature of the early twentieth century.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze cut brownies individually wrapped. Defrost at room temperature for 1–2 hours.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Brownies improve the day after baking — the centre firms slightly and the flavour deepens. Keep at room temperature for 3 days, or refrigerate for 5 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf. At 25 minutes the top should be set and the edges dry; the centre will still feel soft when pressed. It firms as it cools

glossaryTerms.definition: Brownies are done when the top is set and lightly crackled but the centre still feels soft when pressed. A skewer will not come out clean — it should carry wet crumbs or a thin smear of batter. A clean skewer means the brownie is overbaked and will be dry when cold. The centre firms considerably as the brownies cool in the tin, so removing them from the oven while the centre seems underdone is correct.

content.text: Fudgy brownies are undercooked by conventional standards: the skewer should never come out clean. The batter is simple and quick; the skill is entirely in knowing when to stop baking. Pull them too early and they are liquid; too late and they are dry. The correct point is when the edges are set and the centre responds with a soft tremor when you shake the tin.

Preheat the oven to 170°C fan. Butter and line a 20 cm square cake tin. Melt the {{dark-chocolate}} and {{unsalted-butter}} together in a small saucepan over very low heat, stirring until smooth. Remove from the heat and leave to cool for 5 minutes. Whisk in the {{caster-sugar}} and {{light-brown-sugar}} until combined. Whisk in the {{eggs}} one at a time, then the {{vanilla-extract}}. Fold in the sifted {{plain-flour}}, {{cocoa-powder}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} until just combined.
Pour the batter into the prepared tin and spread level. Bake for 25 minutes. Check for

: the top should be set and the edges dry, but the centre soft. Do not overbake. Cool completely in the tin before cutting — at least 30 minutes. Lift out using the paper lining and cut into 16 squares.
